text:
There was a man named George who lived in a dense forest. He preferred solitude and only ventured out occasionally to gather food. One day, while walking through the underbrush back to his hut, he stumbled upon what appeared to be a human skull. His eyes widened with shock and fear. He knew this wasn't human.

Curiosity sparked, George cautiously circled the object. To his relief, the skull turned out to be not human at all but a statue of a deer. Its antlers were twisted and gnarled, making it appear ancient and fearsome, yet its expression was serene, as if looking straight at him.

Perplexed by the find, George picked up the ancient statue and carried it back to his cozy little home. Over time, he admired his peculiar decoration, the oddity gnawing at him. Days turned into weeks, and weeks into months, but George kept watching the deer-statue. He couldn't shake the feeling it was looking at him with
